statement: Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) and Viðreisn (Reform Party) are the two Althingi parties that formally support EU membership. Samfylkingin has supported EU membership since 2007 and led the 2009 application process while in government. Viðreisn was founded in 2016 partly on a pro-EU platform and has consistently advocated for completing accession negotiations. Together they held approximately 17 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.
source_name: Althingi — Party platforms and election results
caveats: Party positions can evolve. Individual MPs may diverge from the official party line. Seat counts reflect the most recent election and may change with by-elections or party defections.

related_entries.statement: Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party), Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party), Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) oppose EU membership. The Independence Party has historically been the strongest opponent, citing sovereignty and fisheries concerns. The Progressive Party, traditionally representing rural interests, opposes membership primarily over agricultural and fisheries policy. These four parties collectively held approximately 33–35 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

Vinstrihreyfingin – grænt framboð (Left-Green Movement) and Píratar (Pirate Party) do not have unified positions on EU membership. The Left-Greens are internally divided: some members support membership for environmental and social policy alignment, while others oppose it on sovereignty and democratic grounds. The party's official position favours a referendum but does not take a side. Píratar support direct democracy and have backed the referendum process without endorsing a specific outcome, though individual MPs lean both ways.

claims.claim_text: Viðreisn hafi verið stofnuð í þeim tilgangi að koma Íslandi í ESB.

citations.report_title: Flott embætti í boði fyrir ESB ríki

claims.claim_text: Samfylkingin, Viðreisn og Flokkur fólksins hafi sett ESB-aðild aftur á dagskrá með fyrirhugaðri þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu.
